source: Learning Rsync - rsync demo 01: Basic format and the common rsync options

questions.q: What is the basic rsync command format?

options: Recursive copy that preserves attributes -- equivalent to -rlptgoD
Deletes extra files at the target
questions.why: -a = -rlptgoD (recursive, links, perms, times, group, owner, devices); it does not include -H/-A/-X.

options: Shows detailed information about the synchronization

questions.why: -z compresses data in transit to save bandwidth.

questions.q: What is the author's habitual rsync invocation?

questions.why: -H hard links; -l symlinks; -A ACLs; -X chattr/extended attributes.

questions.q: The -o (owner) and -g (group) options work only for...
questions.why: Preserving owner and group requires super-user privileges.

questions.q: On Rocky Linux 8, is the rsync package installed by default?
options: Yes -- the rsync rpm ships by default (e.g. 3.1.3)
No, you must dnf install it
questions.why: Rocky Linux 8 installs the rsync rpm by default.
